projects.name: Developer Portfolio
projects.description: My personal developer portfolio website built from scratch, showcasing my skills, projects, and development journey as a programmer.

details.tagline: A dynamic developer portfolio showcasing projects, skills, and interactive UI design
details.problem: The goal was to create a personal portfolio that not only presents my projects, but also reflects my technical skills through interactive and dynamic user experiences.
features: Terminal-style typing animation for an engaging introduction
Infinite scrolling carousels to display technical stack
Responsive vertical timeline showcasing my programming journey
Dynamic project rendering from a JSON data source
Interactive modals for detailed project breakdowns
Contact form with EmailJS integration and live confirmation display
details.challenges: Implementing smooth infinite carousels and a rotating navigation border without performance issues; dynamically generating and formatting project modals
details.learning: Strengthened my ability to build dynamic front-end systems using JavaScript, improved my understanding of DOM manipulation and event-driven design, and built a foundation for creating more complex, data-driven applications.

projects.name: Edufutbol
projects.description: Edufutbol is a solo web project that explores the history and culture of futbol, designed to educate and engage users through accessible, sports-focused content.

details.tagline: An educational platform exploring the history, culture, and global impact of futbol
details.problem: The goal was to create a centralized, engaging platform to educate users about futbol, covering its history, major events, legendary players, and its differences from American football.
features: Structured content covering history, tournaments, and iconic players
Sidebar navigation for improved content accessibility and page exploration
Regularly updated information on major tournament winners
Custom animated background effect using JavaScript
details.challenges: Researching and verifying accurate, relevant information while presenting it in a dynamic, user-friendly and visually appealing format
details.learning: Improved my ability to design and structure content-heavy websites, strengthened my front-end development skills, and gained experience deploying and maintaining a live site with its own domain.

projects.name: Cinematrix
projects.description: Cinematrix is a clean, clutter-free movie discovery platform that helps users quickly find family-friendly films, explore curated selections, revisit classics, and build a personalized list of favorites.

details.tagline: A streamlined movie discovery website focused on simplicity and family-friendly content
details.problem: The goal was to create a simple and intuitive platform for discovering family-friendly movies without the clutter and complexity of modern streaming interfaces.
features: Randomized movie display for quick discovery on the home page
Dynamic rendering of movie data from a JSON source
Interactive modals displaying detailed movie information
Watchlist functionality using localStorage for persistent user data
User submission form for recommending new movies
details.challenges: Implementing dynamic rendering for movies and modals while utilizing localStorage to reliably add and remove movies from the watchlist without breaking user experience
details.learning: Strengthened my understanding of dynamic DOM manipulation, state persistence using localStorage, and building interactive, data-driven user interfaces with JavaScript.

projects.name: Eternal Quest
projects.description: A C# goal-tracking application that motivates users through a points and ranking system and supports one-time, repeatable, and ongoing goals.

details.tagline: A goal-tracking application that motivates progress through gamification and structured task management
details.problem: The goal was to create an application that helps users stay consistent with their goals by introducing structure and motivation through a points-based progression system.
features: Support for one-time, repeatable, and ongoing goals
Points and ranking system to encourage user motivation
Structured goal tracking with clear progress indicators
User-focused design centered around consistency and habit building
details.challenges: Designing a flexible system that could handle multiple types of goals while implementing a points and ranking structure that felt meaningful and motivating without overcomplicating the user experience
details.learning: Developed a stronger understanding of structuring application logic in C#, improved problem-solving skills around state and progression systems, and gained experience designing software focused on user motivation and behavior.

projects.name: Shark Tides
projects.description: A responsive, educational website that provides beginner-friendly shark facts, images, and a quiz while following the principles of dynamic web design.

details.tagline: An interactive educational website about sharks featuring facts, quizzes, and dynamic content generation
details.problem: The goal was to create an engaging and accessible educational website about sharks that presents information in a dynamic and interactive way for users of all knowledge levels.
features: Responsive educational pages covering shark anatomy, species, and conservation status
Random shark fact generator with localStorage tracking
Interactive quiz form to test user knowledge with scoring feedback
Dynamically generated source cards using JavaScript
Image and fact-based content designed for beginner-friendly learning
details.challenges: Ensuring full responsiveness across different screen sizes while maintaining a clean layout; implementing dynamic content generation for facts and source cards while keeping the user experience smooth and consistent
details.learning: Strengthened my ability to build responsive, content-driven websites using JavaScript, improved my understanding of DOM manipulation and client-side state persistence, and gained experience designing interactive, educational user experiences.

projects.name: Task Force One
projects.description: A Python-based daily planner that allows users to add tasks by date, helping organize and manage daily responsibilities through a simple interface.

details.tagline: A Python-based daily planner for organizing tasks and managing responsibilities by date
details.problem: The goal was to create a simple and efficient way to organize daily tasks, allowing users to plan ahead and manage responsibilities without unnecessary complexity.
features: Add and manage tasks organized by specific dates
Clear and simple interface for daily planning
Structured task organization for improved productivity
Lightweight design focused on usability and efficiency
details.challenges: Designing a system to reliably store and retrieve tasks by date while keeping the interface simple and without introducing unnecessary complexity
details.learning: Improved my ability to design structured application logic in Python, strengthened my understanding of data organization, and gained experience building practical, user-focused tools.

projects.name: Mindfullness
projects.description: A C# application that guides users through structured breathing, reflection, and listing exercises, helping reduce stress and build consistent mindfulness habits.

details.tagline: A guided mindfulness application focused on breathing, reflection, and habit-building exercises
details.problem: The goal was to create a simple application that helps users reduce stress and build consistent mindfulness habits through guided and structured exercises.
features: Guided breathing exercises to promote relaxation
Reflection prompts to encourage mindful thinking
Listing activities to build awareness and consistency
Structured session flow for a focused user experience
details.challenges: Designing a structured flow that guides users smoothly through different exercises while keeping the experience simple; implementing timing and control logic to ensure each activity feels intentional and consistent
details.learning: Strengthened my ability to design user-focused applications in C#, improved my understanding of structured program flow and timing logic, and gained experience building software centered around habit formation and user well-being.

projects.name: Scripture Memorizer
projects.description: A C# application that helps users memorize scriptures through guided exercises that progressively hide words, reinforcing recall and strengthening long-term memory.

details.tagline: A guided memorization tool that reinforces recall by progressively hiding words
details.problem: The goal was to create an effective way to help users memorize text by reinforcing recall through repetition and gradual difficulty increases.
features: Progressive word-hiding system to strengthen memory recall
Guided memorization exercises with increasing difficulty
User-controlled pacing for a personalized learning experience
Structured approach to reinforce long-term retention
details.challenges: Designing a system that progressively hides words while maintaining readability and usability; implementing logic to control difficulty progression in a way that effectively reinforces memory
details.learning: Strengthened my ability to design algorithmic solutions for learning and recall, improved my understanding of progressive systems, and gained experience building applications focused on structured data and pattern reinforcement.

projects.name: UFO Hangman
projects.description: A C++ console game inspired by Hangman, where players guess letters to avoid being abducted by a UFO through progressively revealed ASCII visuals.

details.tagline: A C++ console-based Hangman-style game with UFO-themed ASCII visuals
details.problem: The goal was to create an engaging console-based game that reimagines Hangman with a unique theme while reinforcing fundamental programming concepts.
features: Dynamic ASCII visuals representing UFO abduction stages
Input validation for user guesses
Game loop with win/loss conditions and replayability
details.challenges: Implementing game logic to track guesses and update state accurately across each round while integrating ASCII visuals that respond dynamically to player progress
details.learning: Strengthened my understanding of core programming concepts in C++, including control flow, state management, and input handling, while improving my problem-solving skills through game logic implementation.

projects.name: Daily Journal
projects.description: A C# journaling application that encourages consistent writing by providing daily prompts and automatically recording entries, making reflection more accessible and structured.

details.tagline: A C# journaling application that promotes daily reflection through guided prompts
details.problem: The goal was to make journaling more accessible and consistent by providing structured prompts and an easy way to record daily thoughts.
features: Different prompts to encourage consistent writing
Automatic recording and storage of journal entries
Simple interface focused on reflection without distractions
Structured approach to building a daily journaling habit
details.challenges: Designing a system to reliably store and retrieve journal entries while creating meaningful prompts and structuring the experience to encourage consistent user engagement
details.learning: Strengthened my ability to design user-focused applications in C#, improved my understanding of data persistence and retrieval, and gained experience building structured systems that support consistent user habits.

projects.name: Flappy Bird
projects.description: A Python-based Flappy Bird remake built by following a tutorial, serving as a hands-on exercise in game logic, physics, and user input handling.

details.tagline: A Python-based Flappy Bird clone built to practice game logic, physics, and input handling
details.problem: The goal was to gain hands-on experience with game development fundamentals by recreating a simple arcade-style game using Python.
features: Classic Flappy Bird-style gameplay mechanics
Physics-based movement including gravity and jumping mechanics
Collision detection for pipes and boundaries
Keyboard input handling for player control
details.challenges: Implementing smooth physics-based movement and tuning game difficulty; managing collision detection and game state transitions in a way that felt responsive and consistent
details.learning: Gained practical experience with game loops, physics simulation, and user input handling in Python, while strengthening my understanding of how core game mechanics are structured.

projects.name: Form Validation
projects.description: A simple PHP-based form validation project that ensures accurate and secure user input through server-side validation integrated with HTML forms.

details.tagline: A PHP-based form validation system focused on secure and accurate server-side user input handling
details.problem: The goal was to understand and implement secure form handling by validating user input on the server side to ensure data accuracy and prevent malformed submissions.
features: Server-side form validation using PHP
Input validation for required fields and formatting
Basic error handling and user feedback for invalid inputs
details.challenges: Ensuring reliable validation of user input on the server side while maintaining seamless integration with the HTML form; handling edge cases where user input could bypass client-side restrictions
details.learning: Gained foundational understanding of server-side development with PHP, learned the importance of validating user input for security and data integrity, and strengthened my awareness of full-stack form handling principles.

projects.name: The Coolest Creature
projects.description: My first website built from scratch. An early informational website about sharks featuring beginner-friendly facts and pictures.

details.tagline: An early informational website about sharks built as a foundational web development project
details.problem: The goal was to create a simple, beginner-friendly informational website as an introduction to structuring web content using HTML and CSS.
features: Basic informational pages about sharks
Beginner-friendly facts and educational content
Image-based layouts to support visual learning
Simple static website structure built from scratch
details.challenges: Structuring content effectively using only HTML and CSS while maintaining readability and visual organization; learning how to properly layout information without relying on JavaScript or dynamic tools
details.learning: Gained foundational experience in HTML and CSS, improved understanding of webpage structure and layout design, and learned how to present educational content in a clear and accessible format.

projects.name: The Coolest Creature 2.0
projects.description: A JavaScript-driven informational shark website with interactive forms, user input handling, and a dynamic "days since" calculator made from scratch.

details.tagline: A JavaScript-enhanced informational shark website with interactive features and dynamic user input
details.problem: The goal was to improve upon my original static shark website by introducing interactivity, dynamic content, and user-driven features using JavaScript.
features: Interactive forms with user input handling
Dynamic 'days since' calculator built from scratch
Enhanced informational pages about sharks
JavaScript-driven content updates for improved interactivity
details.challenges: Refactoring a static website into a dynamic one using JavaScript while maintaining structure and usability; implementing accurate time-based calculations and managing user input interactions effectively
details.learning: Strengthened my understanding of JavaScript fundamentals, DOM manipulation, and event handling, while learning how to progressively enhance static websites into interactive web applications.
